mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which immediately pressures equity valuations and produces negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during post-shock recovery periods or produces negative returns when industrial production accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in >60% of cases when monthly CPI exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms synchronized economic expansion, supporting sustained earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative return frequency above 50% over 60-day horizons during synchronized growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment levels during a monetary tightening cycle amplify recession fears and compress equity risk premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The unemployment signal fails to predict negative returns with positive return frequency exceeding 55% specifically during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales reflect immediate consumer spending strength, which directly boosts near-term corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02 across rolling 2-year windows.
